explanation: The phrase "A when B" means that whenever B is true, A must also be true. This translates to a global implication: G (B -> A). Therefore, "prop_0 when prop_1" becomes G (prop_1 -> prop_0).

The statement "The autopilot shall prop_0 when prop_1" means that at any point in time, if prop_1 is true, then prop_0 must also be true at that same point in time. This is a common interpretation of 'A when B' as 'always (B implies A)'.

The phrase 'when prop_1' indicates a condition under which 'prop_0' must be true. This translates to a global implication, meaning that whenever 'prop_1' holds, 'prop_0' must also hold. Therefore, the LTL formula is 'Globally (prop_1 implies prop_0)'.
The phrase 'A when B' implies that A must be true whenever B is true. This translates directly to a material implication where B being true implies A being true.

The statement "A shall happen when B happens" translates to "if B is true, then A must also be true at that same moment." This is a global implication, meaning it must hold true at all times. Therefore, the LTL formula G(prop_1 -> prop_0) correctly captures this relationship, stating that globally, if prop_1 is true, then prop_0 must also be true.

The statement "The autopilot shall prop_0 when prop_1" means that whenever `prop_1` is true, `prop_0` must also be true at that same moment. This relationship must hold globally (at all times). Therefore, it translates to a global implication: `G(prop_1 -> prop_0)`.

The statement "The autopilot shall prop_0 when prop_1" means that whenever prop_1 is true, prop_0 must also be true. The word "shall" implies that this condition must hold globally, at all times. Therefore, the LTL translation is "Globally (prop_1 implies prop_0)".
The statement "The autopilot shall prop_0 when prop_1" means that prop_0 must always be true whenever prop_1 is true. This translates to a global implication: G(prop_1 -> prop_0).

The statement "The autopilot shall prop_0 when prop_1" implies that whenever 'prop_1' is true, 'prop_0' must also be true at that same instant. This is a global implication, meaning it must hold true at all times. Therefore, it translates to 'Globally (prop_1 implies prop_0)'.

The statement "The autopilot shall prop_0 when prop_1" means that at any point in time, if prop_1 is true, then prop_0 must also be true at that same point in time. This is a global implication, meaning it must hold true always.
